outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump calls off Iran strike planned for Tuesday, says he'll hold off 'for a little while — hopefully, maybe, forever'
outlets.summary: CNN covers Trump's stunning announcement that a military strike on Iran was scheduled for May 19 but called off at Gulf states' request, while keeping the Pentagon on a 'moment's notice' war footing.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump postpones Iran attack after Gulf leaders plead for more time; Pentagon remains on standby
outlets.summary: The Post examines the extraordinary dynamics of Trump publicly disclosing a canceled strike, the role of Gulf monarchies in US military decision-making, and what comes next in the 48-72 hour diplomatic window.

outlets.name: CBS News
outlets.headline: Trump says called off scheduled Iran attack after Gulf partners asked for time — live war updates
outlets.summary: CBS News live coverage of Trump's late-night announcement calling off the Iran strike, Iran's 4 overnight missile salvos at Israel, and ongoing diplomatic efforts via Pakistan.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump postpones Iran attack at Gulf allies' request — but military 'ready on a moment's notice'
outlets.summary: Fox News frames the attack postponement as strategic patience, emphasizing Trump's Pentagon readiness order and noting the 48-72 hour window before military options return.

outlets.name: Axios
outlets.headline: Trump says attack on Iran paused after Gulf states' requests — 'serious negotiations' underway
outlets.summary: Axios reports on the Gulf states' intervention with Trump, the NSC Situation Room meeting outcome, and the narrow diplomatic window that now exists before US military action resumes.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Trump holds off on Iran attacks at request of Gulf states — deal possible in days
outlets.summary: The Hill covers the Gulf states' unprecedented direct role in halting a US military operation, noting that Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE all personally appealed to Trump within hours of his 'Clock is Ticking' post.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Trump delays 'scheduled attack' on Iran crediting serious negotiations with Gulf state support
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera provides comprehensive coverage of Trump's announcement, Pakistan's mediation role, Iran's 4 overnight missile salvos at Israel, and the Houthi maritime blockade declaration on Haifa port.

outlets.name: PBS NewsHour
outlets.headline: Trump says he's called off Iran strike planned for Tuesday at request of Gulf allies
outlets.summary: PBS covers the diplomatic intervention by Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE in persuading Trump to pause military action, and what this means for the broader US-Iran negotiating framework.

outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.headline: Trump says he's postponing scheduled attack of Iran — oil rebounds as market eyes diplomatic window
outlets.summary: CNBC focuses on the market reaction to Trump's postponement announcement — Brent rebounded to $111.19 (+1.77%), WTI to $107.35 (+1.52%) as investors priced in continued Hormuz disruption risk premium.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Trump calls off Iran strike; Houthis declare Haifa port blockade; Iran fires 4 missile salvos at Israel
outlets.summary: Times of Israel liveblog covers Trump's canceled strike, 4 Iranian missile salvos overnight (all intercepted), Houthi maritime blockade declaration on Haifa, and NSC Situation Room meeting held May 19.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Trump calls off 'scheduled attack' on Iran at Gulf states' request — diplomatic window now open
outlets.summary: Haaretz analyzes the implications for Israel of Trump's decision to pause military action, Netanyahu's response, and the 48-72 hour negotiating window that Gulf states have created.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: US strike on Iran was 'scheduled for today' — Trump discloses canceled attack in late-night post
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post covers the extraordinary Trump disclosure that an attack was specifically planned for May 19, the Gulf states' intercession, and Israeli security officials' reaction to the delay.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran fires missiles at Israel, rejects US demands on enrichment; Pezeshkian: nuclear rights non-negotiable
outlets.summary: PressTV frames Iran's 4 overnight missile salvos as a 'defensive response,' covers Pezeshkian's declaration that enrichment rights cannot be negotiated away, and calls the Trump postponement 'a victory for Iranian resistance.'

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: Iran's missile force proves operational amid talks; PGSA processing vessel applications
outlets.summary: Tasnim covers Iran's overnight missile strikes on Israel as proof of continued operational capability, and reports on PGSA vessel transit application processing under the formal Hormuz toll system.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian says Iran's nuclear enrichment right cannot be negotiated as US-Iran talks stall
outlets.summary: Iran International provides independent analysis of Pezeshkian's public enrichment rights declaration, noting the contrast with Iran's back-channel talks via Pakistan and the political constraints on any deal.
